strength_a.title: Foundation Strength A (Lower-emphasis Full Body)
strength_a.stimulus: Establish back squat as cycle anchor. Calibrate RPE 6.

structure.description: Bike or row 5 min Z2; 2 rounds of 10 air squats / 8 walking lunges per leg / 30s deadhang.

notes.about: Wk1 calibration. Leave reps in reserve. Foundation is for nervous system patterning, not max load.
notes.week_context: Wk1 RPE 6 → Wk2 RPE 7 → Wk3 RPE 7.5 (set/rep shift).

scaling.beginner: Bodyweight or goblet squat. Bodyweight lunges. Ring rows.

notes.substitutions: No barbell — DB front-rack squat, single-leg DB RDL, DB swing.

structure.description: Row 5 min Z2; 2 rounds 10 push-ups / 10 band pull-aparts / 30s deadhang.

notes.about: Strength B is upper-emphasis but integrates single-leg + grip work. Hyrox demands integration.
notes.block_context: Pull volume builds Wk1 RPE 6 → Wk6 RPE 8 → Wk11 RPE 7.

scaling.beginner: Ring rows, DB bench, light farmer carries.

easy_run.title: Foundation Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: Z2 aerobic base. Easy conversational pace.

structure.description: Z2 work (60-70% max HR, ~8:30-9:30/mi, full-sentence talk-test).

notes.about: Aerobic base. No pace pressure.
notes.week_context: Main Z2 builds linearly Wk1 25 → Wk2 30 → Wk3 35 → Wk4 35 → Wk5 38 → Wk6 40.

scaling.beginner: Run-walk strategy. 4 min run / 1 min walk.

hyrox_movement_pattern.title: Foundation Hyrox Movement Pattern
hyrox_movement_pattern.stimulus: Teach station mechanics at moderate pace before adding fatigue. Foundation-only session type.

structure.description: 6 stations × 5-min rotation (3 min work + 2 min teaching/transition) = 30 min.
details: Station 6: Wall balls — light wall ball OR Open weight (6kg M / 4kg W) only if form is clean. Use lighter ball if target height (3.00m M / 2.74m W) or squat depth is not consistent.
Pick weakness-focused: sled push / wall balls / sled pull / BBJ for runners.

notes.about: Wk1 introduces 6 stations to keep load manageable. Wk2 expands to 8.
notes.week_context: Wk1 6 stations → Wk2 8 stations → Wk3 8 stations + light loads.
notes.block_context: After Wk3, this slot becomes Compromised Running.

scaling.beginner: Stay 6 stations. Lightest loads. Form first.
scaling.advanced: Don't go to Open race weights yet.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ), ring rows (sled pull).

details: Repeat 3-min cycle 8 times (24 min) + 1 free minute = 25 min.
Pacing: 35-45 sec work / 15-25 sec rest. RPE 6.

notes.about: Foundation Engine is base-building. Same session category returns at Helgerud 90-95% max HR in Peak Wk9.
notes.block_context: Engine appears Foundation + Peak only. Drops out of Build.

notes.about: Foundation Wk1 — calibration. Leave reps in reserve. Set baseline pacing.
notes.week_context: Wk1 of Foundation block (3 weeks). Wk1 RPE 6 → Wk2 RPE 7 → Wk3 RPE 7.5.
notes.block_context: Foundation builds aerobic base + nervous system patterning before adding session types in Build.

strength_a.title: Foundation Strength A (RPE 7)
strength_a.stimulus: RPE 7 same set/rep as Wk1. Loads should increase modestly.

structure.description: Back squat: 4×6 @ RPE 7. Rep 6 should feel like 3 reps in reserve.

notes.about: RPE 7 same set/rep as Wk1. Loads should increase modestly.
notes.block_context: Foundation strength progression sets anchor loads.

strength_b.title: Foundation Strength B (RPE 7)
strength_b.stimulus: RPE 7 upper. Pull progression Wk1 RPE 6 → Wk2 RPE 7 → Wk3 RPE 7.5.

notes.about: RPE 7 upper. Pull progression Wk1 RPE 6 → Wk2 RPE 7 → Wk3 RPE 7.5.
notes.week_context: Wk2 of Foundation. Pull volume building.

structure.description: Form drills (optional last 3 min): high knees / A-skips / butt kicks.

notes.about: +5 min from Wk1.

hyrox_movement_pattern.title: Foundation Hyrox Movement Pattern (8-station rotation)
hyrox_movement_pattern.stimulus: Full 8-station rotation. Builds station familiarity systematically.

details: Ski / Sled push (60-70% Open) / Sled pull (light) / BBJ / Row / Farmer carry (40-50m moderate) / Sandbag lunges (50m light) / Wall balls (Open 6/4kg to target).

notes.about: Full 8-station rotation. Builds station familiarity systematically.
notes.week_context: Wk1 6 stations → Wk2 8 stations → Wk3 8 stations + light loads.
notes.block_context: After Wk3, this slot becomes Compromised Running.
notes.substitutions: KB swings, DB lunges, jumping squats, ring rows.

structure.description: 30 min EMOM, 8 cal row / 8 cal ski / 150m run, 10 cycles.
details: Pacing: 35-45 sec work / 15-25 sec rest. RPE 6.

notes.about: +5 min from Wk1.
Foundation Wk2 — settled middle. RPE bumps to 7 across strength.
notes.week_context: Wk2 of Foundation block. RPE bumps to 7. Volumes step up.
notes.block_context: Foundation builds aerobic base + nervous system patterning.

strength_a.title: Foundation Strength A (set/rep shift to 4×5, RPE 7.5)
strength_a.stimulus: Single shift in each direction (volume down 4×6→4×5, RPE up 7→7.5). Clean progression.

structure.description: Walking lunges: 3×10/leg, slightly heavier than Wk2

notes.about: Single shift in each direction (volume down 4×6→4×5, RPE up 7→7.5). Clean progression.
notes.week_context: Wk2 4×6 RPE 7 → Wk3 4×5 RPE 7.5 → Wk4 4×5 RPE 7 (Build start, slight de-load for new sessions).

strength_b.title: Foundation Strength B (4×5, RPE 7.5)
strength_b.stimulus: Pull shifts to 4×6 (intensity holds, set count rises).

notes.about: Pull shifts to 4×6 (intensity holds, set count rises).

easy_run.title: Foundation Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: 35 min main work. Foundation Easy Run peak.

notes.about: 35 min main work. Foundation Easy Run peak.

hyrox_movement_pattern.title: Foundation Hyrox Movement Pattern (light loads added)
hyrox_movement_pattern.stimulus: Last week before stations get done under fatigue.

structure.description: 8 stations × 5-min rotation = 40 min. Loads bumped to 70-80% Open weights (vs Wk2's 60-70%).

notes.about: Last week before stations get done under fatigue.
notes.week_context: Wk3 stations + light loads. Wk4 onwards = Compromised Running replaces.

engine.title: Foundation Engine
engine.stimulus: Foundation Engine peak. +10% volume from Wk2 (vs original v2.1's +20% double-ramp).

structure.description: 33 min EMOM (11 cycles × 3 min), 9 cal row / 9 cal ski / 175m run.

notes.about: Foundation Engine peak. +10% volume from Wk2.
notes.week_context: Wk1 25 → Wk2 30 → Wk3 33. Drops out Wk4-6. Returns Wk7 at RPE 7.
notes.block_context: Foundation Engine peak before Build drop-out.

notes.about: Foundation Wk3 — block peak. Set/rep shifts to 4×5 RPE 7.5. Final Movement Pattern week before Build introduces Compromised Running.
notes.week_context: Wk3 of Foundation block. Block peak.
notes.block_context: Foundation closes here. Build (Wk4-6) introduces Recovery Run, Tempo Run, Compromised Running.

strength_a.title: Build Strength A (RPE 7, single-leg work introduced)
strength_a.stimulus: Wk4 introduces Copenhagen plank and single-leg RDL replaces bilateral. Hyrox movements are single-leg dominant.

notes.about: Wk4 introduces Copenhagen plank and single-leg RDL replaces bilateral. Hyrox movements are single-leg dominant.
notes.week_context: Wk4 4×5 RPE 7 → Wk5 RPE 7.5 → Wk6 RPE 8.
notes.block_context: Build supports Wk6 peak (RPE 8) and Wk9 apex.

strength_b.title: Build Strength B (RPE 7, push press introduced)
strength_b.stimulus: Push press over bench — overhead drive translates to wall balls. Pull volume bumps to 4×8.

notes.about: Push press over bench — overhead drive translates to wall balls. Pull volume bumps to 4×8.
notes.week_context: Wk4 of Build. Push press introduced.
notes.block_context: Build introduces overhead drive for wall ball transfer.

easy_run.title: Build Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: Holds 35 min from Wk3 — Build start has new session types so Easy Run holds before resuming +3-5/wk progression.

notes.about: Holds 35 min from Wk3 — Build start has new session types so Easy Run holds.

recovery_run.title: Build Recovery Run (NEW session type)
recovery_run.stimulus: Active recovery, distinct from Easy Run by HR ceiling.

structure.description: Z1-Z2 recovery-paced. Cap at 65% max HR. Slower than Easy Run pace. Walking sections fine.

notes.about: Distinct from Easy Run by HR ceiling (65% max HR cap vs Easy Run's 60-70% range) and pacing language. Same execution at same pace = recovery effect disappears.
notes.week_context: Wk4 25-30 min. Stays 25-35 min through cycle.
notes.block_context: Best placed day after Tempo, Compromised Running, or Sim. Active flush.

scaling.advanced: Hardest scaling — resist running fast. The 65% max HR ceiling is the objective check.

notes.substitutions: Easy hike, swim Z1, light bike Z1.

structure.description: Walk-to-jog Z1, build to Z2 over 8 min, finish with 4×30s strides at tempo with 30s walk.
3×6 min @ Z3 (70-80% max HR, 'comfortably hard'). Rest 2 min easy jog. 18 min total.

notes.about: Tempo intros conservative volume. Z3 is fastest pace held steady.
notes.week_context: Wk4 18 → Wk5 24 → Wk6 25 → Wk7 deload 12 → Wk8 18 → Wk9 30 (peak) → Wk10 15 → Wk11 15 → Wk12 9.

compromised_running.title: Build Compromised Running (Aaron pattern, NEW session type)
compromised_running.stimulus: Train pace under station fatigue. Aaron 4-min run + 2-min station pattern.

structure.description: 4 rounds (4 min run @ tempo + 2 min station). 24 min total work.

details: Stations: sled push / wall balls / sandbag lunges.

notes.about: Aaron pattern — 4-min run / 2-min station rhythm. Pacing is the variable.
notes.week_context: Wk4 4 → Wk5 5 → Wk6 6 → Wk7 deload (no Compromised — see Wk7 architectural note).
notes.block_context: Aaron pattern (rhythm) Build → Welling pattern (race format) Peak.

scaling.advanced: 4 rounds Open weights, Pro for Pro athletes.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

notes.about: Build Wk4 — block start. Recovery Run, Tempo Run, and Compromised Running (Aaron pattern) introduced. Movement Pattern and Engine drop out.
notes.week_context: Wk4 of Build block (3 weeks). New session types introduced.
notes.block_context: Build supports Wk6 peak (RPE 8) and Wk9 apex.

strength_a.title: Build Strength A (RPE 7.5)
strength_a.stimulus: Bridge between Wk4 RPE 7 and Wk6 RPE 8.

notes.about: Bridge between Wk4 RPE 7 and Wk6 RPE 8.

strength_b.title: Build Strength B (RPE 7.5)
strength_b.stimulus: Push press progresses. Pull holds 4×8 high volume.

notes.about: Push press progresses. Pull holds 4×8 high volume.
notes.substitutions: DB push press, KB rack carry.

easy_run.title: Build Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: 38 min main Z2 — corrects v2's progression break.

notes.about: 38 min main Z2.
27 min main Z1-Z2.

structure.description: 4×6 min @ Z3, 2 min easy jog rest. 24 min total.

notes.about: 24 min total tempo.

compromised_running.title: Build Compromised Running (Aaron, sled pull included)

structure.description: 5 rounds (4 min run + 2 min station). 30 min total work.

details: Stations: farmer carry / BBJ / sled pull.

notes.about: 5 rounds with sled pull included.
notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), ring rows (sled pull), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

notes.about: Build Wk5 — settled middle. Progressions across all session types.
notes.week_context: Wk5 of Build block. Sled pull added to Compromised.

strength_a.title: Build Strength A (RPE 8 peak)
strength_a.stimulus: Wk6 peak. RPE 8 = rep 5 challenging, 1-2 reps in reserve. If fatigued, drop to RPE 7.5.

structure.description: Walking lunges with DBs: 3×12/leg, heaviest of Build

notes.about: Wk6 peak. RPE 8 = rep 5 challenging, 1-2 reps in reserve. If fatigued, drop to RPE 7.5.
notes.week_context: Wk6 RPE 8 → Wk7 deload 3×5 RPE 7. Wk9 apex returns to 4×5 RPE 8 (with progressive load — same set/rep, more weight).
notes.collision_warning: Don't stack Strength A, Tempo, and Compromised Running on consecutive days. If fatigue is high, drop Strength A to RPE 7.5 OR cut Compromised by 1 round.

notes.about: Push press peaks RPE 8.

easy_run.title: Build Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: 40 min main Z2. Build's longest run.

notes.about: 40 min main Z2. Build's longest run.
30 min Z1-Z2.

tempo_run.title: Build Tempo Run (5×5 = 25 min)
tempo_run.stimulus: Smoothed from v2's 4×7. Wk5→Wk6 ramp now +4% (was +17%).

structure.description: 5×5 min @ Z3, 90s easy jog rest. 25 min total.

notes.about: Smoothed from v2's 4×7. Wk5→Wk6 ramp now +4%.
notes.collision_warning: Don't stack Strength A, Tempo, and Compromised Running on consecutive days. If fatigue is high, drop Strength A to RPE 7.5 OR cut Compromised by 1 round.

compromised_running.title: Build Compromised Running (Aaron 6 rounds peak)
compromised_running.stimulus: Compromised peak = 6 rounds covering all 6 station patterns.

structure.description: 6 rounds (4 min run + 2 min station). 36 min total work.
details: Stations rotate full set: sled push / wall balls / sled pull / sandbag lunges / farmer carry / BBJ.
Stations: Sled Push / Sled Pull / BBJ.

notes.about: Compromised peak = 6 rounds covering all 6 station patterns.
notes.block_context: Aaron pattern peak. Welling pattern arrives Wk9.
notes.collision_warning: Don't stack Strength A, Tempo, and Compromised Running on consecutive days. If fatigue is high, drop Strength A to RPE 7.5 OR cut Compromised by 1 round.

notes.about: Build Wk6 — Build peak. RPE 8 strength, 6-round Compromised. First high-load week. If legs are heavy, keep strength at RPE 7.5 OR reduce Compromised by 1 round. Wk7 mini-deload follows.
notes.block_context: Build closes here. Wk7 = Peak block opener (mini-deload).
notes.collision_warning: Don't stack Strength A, Tempo, and Compromised Running on consecutive days. If fatigue is high, drop Strength A to RPE 7.5 OR cut Compromised by 1 round.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk7
weeks.summary: Mini-deload week. Compromised dropped, Engine reintroduced.

strength_a.title: Peak Strength A (mini-deload, RPE 7)
strength_a.stimulus: Set count drops 4×5 → 3×5. RPE 8 → 7. Net volume cut ~25-30%.

notes.about: Set count drops 4×5 → 3×5. RPE 8 → 7. Net volume cut ~25-30%.
notes.week_context: Wk7 mini-deload. Wk8 returns to RPE 7-7.5 for sim, Wk9 returns to RPE 8 apex.
notes.block_context: Peak block opener — mini-deload before Wk8 sim and Wk9 apex.

notes.about: Pattern matches Strength A.

easy_run.title: Peak Easy Run
easy_run.stimulus: 35 min main Z2 — drops from Wk6's 40.

notes.about: 35 min main Z2 — drops from Wk6's 40.
27 min Z1-Z2.

structure.description: 3×4 min @ Z3, 2 min rest. 12 min total.

notes.about: Tempo deload — half Wk6's volume.

engine.title: Peak Engine (REINTRODUCED, RPE 7, 9/9/175m)
engine.stimulus: Engine returns at RPE 7 — bridge between Foundation RPE 6 and Wk9 Helgerud.

structure.description: 25 min EMOM rotating 3 modalities at RPE 7.
details: Repeat 3-min cycle 8 times = 24 min, plus 1 free minute = 25 min.
Pacing: RPE 7 — harder than Foundation, easier than Wk9 Helgerud.

notes.about: v2.2 corrected per-minute volume to match Foundation peak. Higher per-minute than Foundation contradicted mini-deload intent.
notes.week_context: Foundation RPE 6 → Build drop-out → Wk7 RPE 7 (re-acclimation) → Wk9 Helgerud 90-95% max HR.

notes.about: Peak Wk7 — mini-deload (Peak block opener). Volume cut ~25-30%, intensity preserved at moderate. Engine reintroduced at RPE 7. Compromised Running dropped this week intentionally.
notes.week_context: Wk7 of Peak block (4 weeks). Architectural: Engine is the 6th session because Wk7 is the only place in the cycle where Engine returns at moderate intensity (RPE 7) bridging Foundation RPE 6 → Wk9 Helgerud.
notes.block_context: Peak block opener. Wk8 Half-Sim, Wk9 apex, Wk10 Full Sim follow.

strength_a.title: Peak Strength A (RPE 7-7.5, lighter for sim)

notes.about: Light to allow Half-Sim performance.
notes.collision_warning: Schedule 2-3 days before Half-Sim, not the day before.

strength_b.title: Peak Strength B (RPE 7-7.5)
strength_b.stimulus: Strength B holds light for sim.

notes.about: Strength B holds light for sim.
30 min main Z2.
27 min Z1-Z2.

tempo_run.title: Peak Tempo Run
tempo_run.stimulus: Build-back from Wk7 deload toward Wk9 peak.

structure.description: 3×6 min @ Z3, 2 min easy jog rest. 18 min total.

notes.about: Build-back from Wk7 deload toward Wk9 peak.

simulation.title: Peak Priority Station Half-Sim
simulation.stimulus: First race-effort simulation. 4 priority stations at 80% race effort. Selection prioritizes race-critical coverage over literal race order.

structure.description: Jog or bike 5 min Z2 + dynamic mobility 3 min.
4 stations × 1 km run + station, in this order.
details: Run 1 km @ race pace (target 70-80% race effort).
Run 1 km. Station 2: Sled push 50m @ Open M 152kg / W 102kg race weight.
Run 1 km. Station 3: Sandbag lunges 100m @ Open M 20kg / W 10kg.
Run 1 km. Station 4: Wall balls — Open M 100 reps @ 6kg to 3.00m / Open W 75 reps @ 4kg to 2.74m / Pro M 100 @ 9kg to 3.00m / Pro W 100 @ 6kg to 2.74m.
At 80% race effort throughout. ~30-35 min total work.

structure.description: 3 stations × 1 km + station: ski / sled push / wall balls. Cuts sandbag if time-constrained.

notes.about: Renamed from 'Half-Hyrox Simulation' to 'Priority Station Half-Sim' since selection covers cardio (ski) + heavy (sled push) + locomotion (sandbag) + race-final (wall balls). Race-critical priority over literal race order. The literal race order half (stations 1-4 = ski, sled push, sled pull, BBJ) skips wall ball pacing exposure entirely, which is higher priority pre-Wk10 full sim.
notes.week_context: First sim. Wk10 Full Sim follows.
notes.block_context: Wk8 Priority Half-Sim → Wk9 apex training → Wk10 Full Sim → Wk11 sharpening.
notes.collision_warning: Half-Sim is priority. Don't place within 48 hrs of Tempo or Strength A.

scaling.beginner: 3 stations (cut sandbag). Sled at 60-70% Open. 75 reps wall balls regardless.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), KB swings (wall balls). Loss of station-specificity, brick effect preserved.

notes.about: Peak Wk8 — Race simulation 1 (Priority Station Half-Sim) at 80% race effort.
notes.week_context: Wk8 of Peak — first sim week.
notes.block_context: First race-effort simulation. Wk10 Full Sim follows.
notes.collision_warning: Half-Sim is priority. Don't place within 48 hrs of Tempo or Strength A.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk9 (APEX)
weeks.summary: Cycle apex. Maximum weekly load. Optional 7th session.

strength_a.title: Peak Strength A (apex, RPE 8, sled pull added)
strength_a.stimulus: Apex lower-body strength. Squat at peak intensity. Deadlift at moderate volume. Sled pull race-critical addition.

structure.description: Back squat: 4×5 @ RPE 8. Apex strength load — same set/rep as Wk6 but higher absolute weight.
Sled pull: 3×50m @ 110-120% race weight (Open M 113-124kg, Open W 86-94kg). Focus: low hip position, hand-over-hand, consistent rhythm.

notes.about: Apex strength session. Same 4×5 RPE 8 as Wk6 Build peak — but if you've trained Wk7-8 correctly, you should be lifting 5-10% more absolute weight than Wk6 at the same RPE 8. This is progressive overload at work — your nervous system adapted across the mini-deload + sim week, and Wk9 expresses that adaptation. The prescription looks identical; the loads should not be. Deadlift introduces moderate volume. Sled pull addresses race-critical gap from round 2 audit — train ABOVE race weight to make race weight feel manageable.
notes.week_context: Wk6 4×5 RPE 8 (Build peak) → Wk7 3×5 RPE 7 (deload) → Wk8 3×5 RPE 7-7.5 (sim) → Wk9 4×5 RPE 8 (apex).
notes.block_context: Apex week. RPE 8 ceiling — don't push to 9.
notes.collision_warning: 24 hrs separation from Welling Compromised and Helgerud Engine (if doing optional 7th).

scaling.beginner: Drop deadlift to RDL. 3×5 squat. Sled pull at 90% Open weight. Band pull-up.
scaling.advanced: Stay RPE 8. Don't max effort on sled pull — train it, don't race it.

notes.substitutions: No sled — heavy back rows. No barbell — DB front-rack squat, DB RDL.

strength_b.title: Peak Strength B (apex, RPE 8, RFESS default)
strength_b.stimulus: Apex upper. Bench at peak. RFESS replaces pistol for unilateral.

structure.description: Rear-foot-elevated split squat (RFESS) with DBs: 3×6/side @ RPE 7.5. Demands knee stability, glute drive, single-leg balance — key Hyrox patterns. Loadable heavier than pistol, much safer under apex fatigue.

notes.about: RFESS replaces pistol per round 2 audit. Pistols require ankle mobility and balance that fail under apex fatigue.

scaling.beginner: RFESS becomes split squat (no rear-foot elevation). Pistol NOT available for beginners. Lat pulldown for pull-ups.
scaling.advanced: RFESS at heavier load. Pistol remains ADVANCED OPTION ONLY for athletes with proven pistol competency under fatigue (3×6/side bodyweight only if choosing pistol).

notes.substitutions: No rear-foot elevation surface — split squat with DBs. No bench — DB floor press.

easy_run.title: Peak Easy Run (peak running volume)
easy_run.stimulus: 50 min main — peak Easy Run distance for Cycle 1.

notes.about: 50 min main — peak Easy Run distance for Cycle 1.

recovery_run.title: Peak Recovery Run
recovery_run.stimulus: 30 min main Z1-Z2 (cap 65%). Recovery Run doesn't need to peak.

notes.about: v2.2 corrected — was macro 35 min vs detail 30 min mismatch. 30 min sufficient even in apex week. Recovery Run doesn't need to peak.
notes.substitutions: Hike, swim Z1, light bike Z1.

tempo_run.title: Peak Tempo Run (peak 30 min, 90s rest)
tempo_run.stimulus: Hardest Z3 session of cycle. 30 min total.

structure.description: 5×6 min @ Z3 (70-80% max HR, comfortably hard). 90s easy jog rest. 30 min total.

notes.about: Hardest Z3 session of cycle. 30 min total.

compromised_running.title: Peak Compromised Running (Welling, race-specific)
compromised_running.stimulus: 5×(1 km + 25 wall balls) — race brick at peak volume.

structure.description: 5 rounds (1 km run @ tempo + 25 wall balls).
details: Wall balls: Open M 6kg to 3.00m, Open W 4kg to 2.74m, Pro M 9kg to 3.00m, Pro W 6kg to 2.74m. 25 reps. AIM UNBROKEN. If you must break, limit to 1 pause (20+5 or 18+7). 2+ breaks = load too heavy or run too hot — slow next round.

notes.about: Welling pattern is race-day specific.
notes.collision_warning: 24 hrs separation from Helgerud Engine and Strength A.

scaling.advanced: 5 rounds Open, Pro for Pro athletes.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (35 reps) for wall balls if no setup.

engine_optional.title: Peak Engine — Helgerud 4×4 + wall ball pacing finisher (OPTIONAL — replaces Recovery Run)
engine_optional.stimulus: Maximum VO2 max stimulus + race-specific wall ball pacing. Optional 7th for athletes recovering well.

structure.description: 5 min Z2 jog/row/bike, 5 min progressive intensity build with 4×30s strides at Z4.
4×4 min at 90-95% max HR (Z5). Rotate modality each interval.
details: Interval 4: 4 min run (close out at strongest modality, push for negative split).
5×20 wall balls @ Open weight, 60s rest between sets.
Time each set. Goal: all 5 sets within 5 seconds of each other.
Log avg reps/min. Teaches sustainable pace for 100-rep race station.

structure.description: 3×4 min @ 90-95% max HR, 2 min active recovery between. Same modality rotation.
Wall ball pacing finisher: 3×20 wall balls @ Open, 60s rest.

notes.about: Helgerud 4×4 — evidence-supported VO2 max protocol. Talk-test during work intervals: 1-2 words max. Wall ball pacing test addresses 100-rep race station gap. 4-min intervals at 90-95% max HR with 3-min active recovery are an evidence-supported VO2 max protocol producing strong VO2 max gains in trained athletes.
notes.week_context: OPTIONAL 7th session. Default Wk9 = skip Engine. Advanced Wk9 = Engine REPLACES Recovery Run (not adds). Don't do all 7 sessions in apex week.
notes.block_context: Foundation Engine RPE 6 → Wk7 reintro RPE 7 → Wk9 Helgerud 90-95% max HR.
notes.collision_warning: 24 hrs separation from Strength A and Compromised Running.

scaling.beginner: 3×3 min single-modality (only run, only ski, OR only row — don't switch modalities at 95% max HR). Wall ball pacing reduced to 3×15 reps.

notes.substitutions: Single-modality 4×4 min run intervals = valid Helgerud session.

notes.about: Peak Wk9 — APEX. Cycle's hardest week. Maximum running volume, peak tempo, optional Helgerud Engine, 5 rounds Welling Compromised, plus apex strength load. Same set/rep as Wk6 strength but higher absolute load (progressive overload at constant RPE 8).
notes.week_context: Default Wk9 = 6 sessions (Strength A, Strength B, Easy, Recovery, Tempo, Compromised). Most athletes skip Engine. Advanced Wk9 = Engine REPLACES Recovery Run, not adds to it.
notes.block_context: Apex week. Peak Z2, Tempo, and Compromised all peak here.
notes.collision_warning: Spread Tempo, Helgerud Engine (if doing optional 7th), and Compromised Running across the week with at least 1 rest or easy day between each. If scheduling conflicts arise, drop Engine to RPE 7 (Foundation level) rather than executing all three at full prescription within 48 hours.

notes.about: Strength reduces for sim performance.
RFESS holds for unilateral capacity.
30 min main Z2.
27 min Z1-Z2.

structure.description: 3×5 min @ Z3, 2 min rest. 15 min total.

notes.about: Tempo reduced for Full Sim.

simulation.title: Peak Full Hyrox Simulation
simulation.stimulus: Race-effort full simulation. 8 stations in race order at 85-90% race effort.

structure.description: Jog or bike 5 min Z2 + dynamic mobility 5 min.
8 stations × 1 km run + station, in OFFICIAL race order.
details: Run 1 km. Station 2: Sled push 50m @ Open M 152 / W 102.
Run 1 km. Station 3: Sled pull 50m @ Open M 103 / W 78.
Run 1 km. Station 4: Burpee broad jumps 80m.
Run 1 km. Station 6: Farmer carry 200m @ 2×24kg M / 2×16kg W.
Run 1 km. Station 7: Sandbag lunges 100m @ Open M 20 / W 10.
Run 1 km. Station 8: Wall balls — Open M 100 reps @ 6kg to 3.00m / Open W 75 reps @ 4kg to 2.74m / Pro M 100 @ 9kg to 3.00m / Pro W 100 @ 6kg to 2.74m.
Total 75-105 min for most intermediate athletes; faster athletes 60-75 min.

structure.description: 4 stations × 1 km run + station, in this order.
details: Row 1000m → Sled Pull 50m @ Open M 103 / W 78 → Farmer Carry 200m → BBJ 80m.

Quick Rox covers BACK-HALF stations not duplicated by Wk8 Half-Sim. With Wk8 (ski + sled push + sandbag + wall balls) + Wk10 (row + sled pull + farmer + BBJ) = full 8-station coverage for Quick Rox users.

notes.about: Race order matters — body learns actual race sequence and transition patterns. 85-90% effort, not 100% — leaves room for better pacing on race day.
notes.week_context: Wk8 Half-Sim 80% → Wk10 Full Sim 85-90%. Two race-effort exposures.
notes.block_context: After Wk10, Race Prep begins (sharpening Wk11, deload/race Wk12).
notes.collision_warning: Schedule Full Sim with at least 48 hrs recovery before AND after.

scaling.beginner: 6 stations (cut sled pull and BBJ). Open weights at 70-80%. Wall balls at 75 reps regardless.
scaling.advanced: 8 stations Open or Pro weights. Time the sim — use as race day benchmark.

notes.substitutions: No Hyrox kit — KB swings (sled push), ring rows (sled pull), jumping squats (BBJ), DB lunges (sandbag), KB swings (wall balls). Race-specificity lost; brick effect preserved.

notes.about: Peak Wk10 — Race simulation 2 (Full Sim) at 85-90% race effort. Last week of Peak block.
notes.week_context: Wk10 of Peak — second sim week.
notes.block_context: Peak block closes here. Race Prep (Wk11-12) begins.
notes.collision_warning: Schedule Full Sim with at least 48 hrs recovery before AND after.

strength_a.title: Race Prep Strength A (sharpening, 3×4 RPE 7)
strength_a.stimulus: Sharpening preserves intensity (RPE 7 moderate-heavy), cuts volume.

structure.description: Back squat: 3×4 @ RPE 7. Volume cut 40% from Wk9's 4×5.

notes.about: Sharpening preserves intensity (RPE 7 moderate-heavy), cuts volume.
notes.week_context: Wk11 of Race Prep — sharpening. Wk12 = race week / structural deload.

strength_b.title: Race Prep Strength B (3×4 RPE 7)
strength_b.stimulus: Volume cut on all movements. RPE 7 preserves neural readiness.

notes.about: Volume cut on all movements. RPE 7 preserves neural readiness.
30 min main — down from Wk9's 50.

tempo_run.title: Race Prep Tempo Run (sharpening, 3×5 = 15 min)
tempo_run.stimulus: Strict descending taper. Wk9 30 → Wk10 15 → Wk11 15 → Wk12 9. Tempo intensity preserved (Z3), volume held flat from Wk10 to maintain clean taper curve.

structure.description: 3×5 min @ Z3, 2 min easy jog rest. 15 min total.

notes.about: Strict descending taper per Mujika & Padilla taper meta-analysis: strict volume reduction with intensity preservation is the proven taper protocol.

compromised_running.title: Race Prep Compromised Running (Welling, 3 rounds)
compromised_running.stimulus: Sharpening Welling. 3 rounds vs Wk9's 5. Pattern preserved, volume cut 40%.

structure.description: 3 rounds (1 km run @ tempo + 25 wall balls).
details: Wall balls: aim unbroken, 1 break max.

notes.about: Sharpening Welling. 3 rounds vs Wk9's 5. Pattern preserved, volume cut 40%.

scaling.advanced: 3 rounds Open, Pro for Pro athletes.

notes.about: Race Prep Wk11 — sharpening. Volume reduced 30-40% from Wk9 apex, intensity preserved. You're maintaining neural readiness.
notes.week_context: Wk11 of Race Prep block (2 weeks). Wk12 = race week or structural deload.
notes.block_context: Race Prep sharpening before Wk12 divergence.
notes.collision_warning: If feeling fatigued, drop one session entirely. Drop priority order: (1) Keep Tempo and Compromised Running (core sharpening). (2) Drop Recovery Run if short on time. (3) Drop Easy Run last. (4) Don't drop Tempo or Compromised unless injury risk.

racer.label: Racer (race this week)
racer.intro: 3-4 sessions total. Skip ALL other session types — Recovery Run, Engine, Compromised Running, extra Strength. Rest days are part of the taper.
racer.sequencing: If race is 6 days out: Day -6 optional Strength → Day -5 Tempo → Day -4 Easy → Day -3 rest → Day -2 rest → Day -1 rest → Race Day. Adjust window proportionally for shorter notice.

race_prep_strength_optional.title: Race Prep Strength (optional)
race_prep_strength_optional.stimulus: Light strength session. Execute Day -6 or Day -5 only if feeling fresh. Skip if any doubt or if race is Friday-Sunday.

structure.description: Back squat: 2×3 @ RPE 6. Neural maintenance only.

notes.about: Light strength session. Execute Day -6 or Day -5 only if feeling fresh. Skip if any doubt or if race is Friday-Sunday. Day -4 or later: SKIP entirely.
notes.collision_warning: If racing this weekend, do not perform this session.

scaling.advanced: 2×3 RPE 6 max — discipline check, not strength training.

race_prep_tempo.title: Race Prep Tempo (3×3 min sharp)
race_prep_tempo.stimulus: Final pre-race tempo sharpener. Day -5 or Day -4.

structure.description: Walk-to-jog Z1 → Z2 build + 3×30s strides at tempo with 30s walk.
3×3 min @ Z3 (70-80% max HR). Rest 2 min easy jog. 9 min total.

notes.about: Race-week sharpener. Sharp but short. Keep tempo pace memory active without taxing recovery.
notes.week_context: Day -5 or Day -4 (NEVER within 2 days of race day).
notes.collision_warning: Don't do within 2 days of race day.

race_prep_easy_run.title: Race Prep Easy Run (mid-week, sharp)
race_prep_easy_run.stimulus: Day -4 or Day -3. Keep legs primed.

notes.about: Day -4 or Day -3. Keep legs primed.

structure.description: If racing this weekend: race execution at 100% effort. Trust the taper. The race is your training expressed.
If race delayed/rescheduled: final 80% sim using Wk10 Full Sim structure as template.
Race execution at 100% effort. Trust the taper.

notes.about: Race day or final 80% sim if race delayed.
notes.block_context: The race is your training expressed.

continuous.label: Continuous trainee (no race)
continuous.intro: Structural deload. 11 weeks of training (no mid-cycle full deload). This week is structural deload before Cycle 2.

strength_a.title: Continuous Strength A (deep deload, RPE 5)
strength_a.stimulus: Structural deload. RPE 5 = leaving 5 reps in reserve. Keeping movement patterns active.

structure.description: Walking lunges: 2×6/leg bodyweight or light DBs

notes.about: Structural deload. RPE 5 = leaving 5 reps in reserve. Keeping movement patterns active.
notes.week_context: 11 weeks of training (no mid-cycle full deload). Wk12 is structural deload before Cycle 2.

scaling.advanced: Stay RPE 5. Resist 'just one heavy set.'

notes.about: Light upper deload.
22 min Z2 — short deload run.

recovery_run.title: Continuous Recovery Run
recovery_run.stimulus: 20 min Z1-low Z2 — Recovery Run shortest of cycle.

structure.description: 18 min Z1-low Z2, preferably below 60% max HR, hard cap 65%.

notes.about: 20 min Z1-low Z2 — Recovery Run shortest of cycle.

continuous_light_emom.title: Continuous Light EMOM
continuous_light_emom.stimulus: Light station maintenance. RPE 5-6. Movement maintenance, not training stimulus.

structure.description: 20 min light station review. Rotate 4 stations × 5 min each at RPE 5-6.
details: Station 1: Light wall balls (lower than race weight, focus on form), 15-20 reps.
Station 2: Light sled push (50% Open weight or less), or substitute with KB swings 15 reps.

structure.description: 20 min light station review (already short — same as Full Rox).

notes.about: Final session of Cycle 1 for continuous trainees. Light, slow, technical. Keeps station patterns active before Cycle 2.
notes.week_context: Replaces sims/Compromised Running for continuous trainees.

scaling.beginner: Stay 3-station EMOM (drop wall balls). Lightest loads.

notes.substitutions: Single-modality EMOM (e.g., 20 min light row at RPE 5) is valid if equipment limited. Default to wall balls or air squats over jumping squats — avoid impact on fatigued joints during deload.

notes.about: Race week / structural deload. Single week where the cycle's framing-only-difference rule breaks: race week emphasizes neural priming + glycogen super-comp; structural deload emphasizes systemic fatigue clearance.
notes.week_context: Cycle close. Pick the variant that matches your situation.
notes.block_context: Race Prep close. Cycle 2 begins after Wk12.
